description: PRD-Scoped Sessions & Concurrent Loop Support: scope all task queries by PRD prefix, enable concurrent loop sessions with per-PRD locks, per-session signal files, and scoped --force reinit.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Anticipate edge cases. Tests verify boundaries work correctly
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Pragmatic, reliable code that works according to the plan
3. CORRECTNESS - Code compiles, type-checks, passes all tests deterministically
4. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Ship solid working code first, then use tests to improve it
Test the boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: Test escape_like: % → \%, _ → \_, \ → \\
Test prefix_where with Some('P1') returns ("WHERE id LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\'", Some("P1-%"))
Test prefix_where with None returns ("", None)
Test prefix_and with Some('P1') returns ("AND id LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\'", Some("P1-%"))
Test prefix_and with None returns ("", None)
Test validate_prefix accepts [a-zA-Z0-9._-] chars
Test validate_prefix rejects %, _, /, \, spaces, and empty string
Known-bad discriminator: prefix containing underscore literal must be escaped (naive impl would match unintended IDs)
Discriminator: prefix_where('P1') must NOT produce a pattern that matches 'P1-extra-US-001' when only 'P1-US-001' tasks are intended
Test file compiles
userStories.description: Write unit tests for the shared prefix utility module BEFORE implementation. Tests cover LIKE escaping, prefix_where/prefix_and clause generation, None passthrough, and prefix validation. Must include a known-bad discriminator that rejects a naive implementation.
edgeCases: Prefix containing percent sign: 'P%1' must be escaped to 'P\%1'
Prefix containing underscore: 'P_1' must be escaped to 'P\_1'
Prefix containing backslash: 'P\1' must be escaped to 'P\\1'
Empty prefix string should be rejected by validate_prefix
Prefix with only valid chars like 'P1.alpha-2' should pass validation
Prefix 'P1' vs task 'P1-extra-US-001': LIKE pattern 'P1-%' should match P1-extra-US-001 — this is by design since task IDs use PREFIX-STORY-NNN format, but test documents the behavior

failureModes.cause: Prefix with LIKE wildcards not escaped

failureModes.expectedBehavior: validate_prefix returns Err, not passed to filesystem

invariants: prefix_where(None) always returns empty string and None
prefix_and(None) always returns empty string and None
escape_like on a string with no wildcards returns the original string unchanged
userStories.notes: TDD: Write these tests first. Create test module in src/db/prefix.rs (the module will be created in FEAT-001). Tests will initially fail until implementation is done.

correctness: LIKE wildcards (%, _, \) must be escaped to prevent unintended matches
validate_prefix must reject filesystem-unsafe chars
style: Follow existing test patterns: use assert_eq! for exact string comparisons

userStories.title: Initial tests for prefix utility module (escape_like, prefix_where, prefix_and, validate_prefix)

acceptanceCriteria: Test up migration preserves existing prd_metadata row

Test task_prefix UNIQUE constraint prevents duplicate prefixes
Test down migration recreates CHECK(id=1) and copies first row back
Test schema_version updated to 9 after up migration
Known-bad discriminator: inserting id=2 must succeed after migration (would fail if CHECK still present)

Tests verify that after migration: (1) CHECK(id=1) constraint is removed, (2) existing data is preserved, (3) task_prefix has UNIQUE constraint, (4) AUTOINCREMENT works for new rows, (5) down migration restores singleton constraint.

Inserting two rows with same task_prefix must fail (UNIQUE constraint)
Down migration when multiple rows exist: only first row (ORDER BY id ASC) is preserved

failureModes.expectedBehavior: INSERT INTO ... SELECT * preserves all columns and rows

invariants: After up migration: prd_metadata no longer has CHECK(id=1)
After up migration: existing data is intact
After down migration: prd_metadata has CHECK(id=1) again
schema_version in global_state matches migration version
userStories.notes: TDD: Uses the existing migration test patterns from src/db/migrations/tests.rs. Test applies v9 up migration then verifies constraints changed.

acceptanceCriteria: Test initial recovery only resets P1 in_progress tasks, not P2
Test remaining count only counts P1 tasks, not P2

userStories.description: Comprehensive tests for all engine.rs query scoping: initial recovery, remaining count, mid-loop recovery, reconciliation. Test with two PRDs (P1, P2) in same DB to verify isolation.
userStories.notes: Create test fixtures with two PRDs (P1-*, P2-*) and verify each engine query respects prefix boundaries.

correctness: Every scoped query must be tested with cross-PRD data
style: Use assert_eq! for exact comparisons, not just assert!(contains)

userStories.title: Comprehensive tests for prefix-scoped engine queries

acceptanceCriteria: Test .stop-P1 only stops P1 session (P2 unaffected)
Test .pause-P1 only pauses P1 session (P2 unaffected)
Test global .stop stops BOTH P1 and P2 sessions
Test global .pause pauses BOTH P1 and P2 sessions
Test cleanup with P1 prefix: .stop-P1 removed, .stop-P2 and .stop preserved
Test cleanup with None prefix: .stop and .pause removed (legacy behavior)
Test lock file naming: loop-P1.lock for prefix P1, loop.lock for None
Test concurrent lock: acquire lock for P1 and P2 concurrently — both succeed
Test same-PRD lock: acquire lock for P1 twice — second fails with clear error message
80%+ coverage for signal functions
userStories.description: Comprehensive tests for signal file behavior with prefixes: session-specific signals, global fallback, cleanup isolation. Test lock file naming and concurrent acquisition.
userStories.notes: Use tempfile::TempDir for filesystem tests. Create all signal file variants and verify correct behavior for each scenario. For lock tests, use LockGuard::acquire_named() directly.

correctness: Signal isolation between sessions is critical for concurrent support
Lock isolation: different prefixes succeed, same prefix fails
style: Follow existing signal test patterns in signals.rs

userStories.title: Comprehensive tests for per-session signal files and lock files

acceptanceCriteria: Test scoring: P1 task with high file overlap selected over P1 task with low overlap
Test dependency: P1 task blocked by P2 dependency is NOT blocked (cross-PRD deps ignored when prefix is set)
Test synergy: P1 recently-completed provides synergy bonus only to P1 tasks
Test decay: P1 decay only resets P1 blocked/skipped tasks
Test batch: batchWith from P1 to P2 is ignored when prefix is set
Test select with None prefix: all tasks eligible (backwards compat)
Test single-PRD DB with no prefix: identical behavior to current implementation

userStories.description: Comprehensive tests for select_next_task with a database containing tasks from multiple PRDs. Verify scoring, dependency checking, batch grouping, and decay all respect prefix boundaries.
userStories.notes: Create a rich test fixture with P1 and P2 tasks having dependencies, synergies, conflicts, and batch relationships across prefix boundaries.

correctness: Cross-PRD dependencies/synergies/conflicts must not affect selection when prefix is set
style: Use clear test names describing the cross-PRD scenario

userStories.title: Comprehensive tests for task selection with mixed-PRD database
